text:
No. There was no court finding in the Fox–Dominion case that validated the allegation that Dominion manipulated vote totals.

Bottom line:
- The case ended in a settlement before trial.
- The judge made pretrial rulings that the challenged fraud claims about Dominion were false for purposes of the defamation case.
- So there was no merits finding that Dominion had altered vote totals, and no evidentiary airing at trial that established such a claim.

If you want the careful formulation: the case did not establish that Dominion manipulated votes, and it produced no judicial validation of that allegation. At the same time, because it settled before trial, it also did not produce a full trial record resolving every factual contention people might have wanted explored.
